template:
defmodule Solution do
  @doc """
  Decompresses a run-length encoded string with nested brackets.
  """
  def decompress(s) do
    {result, _} = parse(s, 0)
    result
  end

  defp parse(s, pos) when pos >= String.length(s), do: {"", pos}

  defp parse(s, pos) do
    parse_segments(s, pos, "")
  end

  defp parse_segments(s, pos, acc) when pos >= String.length(s), do: {acc, pos}

  defp parse_segments(s, pos, acc) do
    char = String.at(s, pos)

    cond do
      [[SLOT close_bracket_check]] ->
        {acc, pos}

      [[SLOT digit_check]] ->
        {num, new_pos} = parse_number(s, pos)
        # Skip opening bracket
        new_pos = new_pos + 1
        {content, after_bracket} = parse(s, new_pos)
        # Skip closing bracket
        after_bracket = after_bracket + 1
        repeated = [[SLOT repeat_call]]
        parse_segments(s, after_bracket, acc <> repeated)

      true ->
        parse_segments(s, pos + 1, acc <> char)
    end
  end

  defp parse_number(s, pos) do
    parse_number(s, pos, 0)
  end

  defp parse_number(s, pos, acc) do
    if pos < String.length(s) do
      char = String.at(s, pos)
      if char >= "0" and char <= "9" do
        digit = [[SLOT to_int_call]]
        parse_number(s, pos + 1, acc * 10 + digit)
      else
        {acc, pos}
      end
    else
      {acc, pos}
    end
  end
end
prompt: Pick the bracket predicate, digit range check, repeat call, and digit-to-int parse.
